exemplar.title: Investigating the Effect of The Rhizophagus intraradices Fungus on Nutrient Uptake and Growth in Mentha spicata Plants under Different Phosphorus Conditions

detailedExemplarData.title: Investigating the Effect of The Rhizophagus intraradices Fungus on Nutrient Uptake and Growth in Mentha spicata Plants under Different Phosphorus Conditions

subject.title: Biology
subject.description:
**IB Biology** is a **Group 4** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **four interconnected themes: Unity and Diversity, Form and Function, Interaction and Interdependence, and Continuity and Change**, with a strong focus on **experimental work, scientific inquiry, data analysis, and practical investigation skills**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2025**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Multiple-choice and data-based questions | 1 hour 20 min | 2 hours | 36% |
| **Paper 2** | Short and extended response questions | 1 hour 30 min | 2 hours 30 min | 44% |
| **Scientific Investigation** | Individual research project | 10 hours | 10 hours | 20% |

strengths: Introduction communicates topic accurately with depth and relevance
Research question is clearly stated, focused and operational
Methodology employs an appropriate range of techniques with clear procedural detail
weaknesses: Inconsistency between stated number of pots (12) and actual replicates (15)
Rationale for sample size and statistical power is implied but not explicitly discussed
Minor repetitive phrasing in the introduction
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Two of the three Focus & Method strandsâthe clarity of topic communication and the focus of the research questionâsit firmly in the top band (5â6), while the methodological design shows thoughtful planning but some incompleteness (band 3â4). A holistic mark of 5/6 reflects the overall strength in explanation and question focus, tempered by minor methodological gaps.

strengths: Use of discipline-specific terminology is accurate and consistent throughout
Relevant source material frames the study context and supports interpretation of results
weaknesses: Application of literature remains largely descriptive with limited critical synthesis
Some statements are referenced imprecisely, reducing source-use effectiveness
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: While the consistent and correct use of terminology merits the top band (5â6), the application of source material is more descriptive than analytical (band 3â4). An overall score of 5/6 acknowledges secure understanding and terminology use, with room for deeper source integration and critique.

strengths: Data analysis is competently performed with clear linkage to the research question
Statistical methods (ANOVA, Tukey HSD) are correctly applied and interpreted
Arguments are logically constructed and supported by quantitative results
weaknesses: Evaluation of limitations and alternative explanations is somewhat superficial
Key aspects such as effect-size metrics and pH control data are not fully explored
Some minor inconsistencies (e.g., missing RÂ², incomplete risk-assessment protocols) weaken critical depth
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: All three critical-thinking strands (analysis, evaluation, argument development) fall into the middle band (4â6). The analysis is solid and relevant but lacks the sustained critical depth for the highest band. A balanced mark of 5/12 recognizes strong data handling and coherence while noting areas where deeper evaluation and justification are needed.

strengths: Report structure is clear and consistently applied, with logical flow between sections
Layout considerations (font, headings, numbering) are correctly applied throughout
weaknesses: Occasional redundant headings and inconsistent spacing
Several figures and tables lack complete labels or values (e.g., missing error-bar definitions, incomplete calculation steps)
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The presentation meets the highest band (3â4) with an appropriate, reader-friendly structure and correctly applied formatting. Minor redundancies and some incomplete figure/table elements prevent a flawless execution but justify a full 4/4.

strandSelections.reasoning:
**The report adheres to a well-organised EE format** with title page, abstract substitute (implicit), table of contents, sequential chapters and clear sub-headings. Figures, tables and appendices are integrated and referenced, aiding reader navigation. 
â¢ Transitions between sections are generally smooth, and the progression from background to analysis is easy to follow. 
â¢ Minor redundancies and uneven depth across chapters are present but do not disrupt overall clarity, satisfying the top band; hence a score of 4.

**Formal requirements are largely met**: word count, title, page numbering, TOC, consistent font and heading hierarchy, in-text citations and bibliography in a recognisable style. Figures and tables are labelled. 
â¢ Minor issues include some duplicated table captions, occasional inconsistent spacing, and several long blocks of text that reduce readability. Referencing style shows small formatting inconsistencies. 
â¢ Given these mostly correct but not flawless applications, the strand aligns with the 3-4 band; a score of 3 reflects solid but not exemplary execution.

**The essay follows a logical chapter structure (Introduction â Methodology â Data â Conclusion) and links evidence to the central claim that mycorrhizal inoculation enhances growth and P-uptake.** Statistical outcomes are used to support assertions, and counter-arguments (e.g., high P still benefits) are acknowledged. 
â¢ Nonetheless, the argument occasionally drifts into repetition; some claims (e.g., error-bar interpretations) are asserted without full statistical backing, and minor inconsistencies (replicate numbers) weaken coherence. 
â¢ These features indicate a clear but not consistently rigorous argument, corresponding to a score of 4 in the 4-6 band.

**Clear, specific title and detailed background** (ecology of arbuscular-mycorrhizae, phosphorus physiology, spectrophotometry) ensure the reader immediately understands what is being investigated and why it matters for sustainable agriculture. 
â¢ The introduction explains the biological context, agronomic relevance and anticipated applications, showing **breadth and depth of explanation** beyond a simple statement of purpose. 
â¢ Minor issuesâsome repetition and wording lapsesâprevent a flawless presentation, so the strand fits solidly in the 5-6 band but closer to 5 than 6.

**Literature is generally relevant and used to frame the study and interpret findings** (e.g., Smith & Read 2008, strigolactone signalling, AMF efficiency). In the discussion the student links results to published uptake percentages, demonstrating understanding of context. 
â¢ Nevertheless, many citations remain descriptive background; source material is seldom critiqued or synthesised to build deeper insight, and some statements are referenced imprecisely. 
â¢ Overall the application is more than limited but not consistently sophisticated, so the work aligns with the 3-4 band, earning 4.

**Data handling is competent: three growth trials, extensive spectrophotometry (n=15), means, SD, ANOVA and Tukey HSD performed.** Figures and tables are clear, with sample calculations and error analysis. 
â¢ Analysis centres on the RQ, comparing treatments and interpreting trends logically, demonstrating **good linkage between data and biological reasoning**. 
â¢ Yet, deeper exploration of alternative explanations (e.g., duration effects, potential confounders) is limited, keeping the work within the 4-6 band; quality merits a solid 5.

**Method covers variables, materials, detailed procedures, risk assessment and statistics, indicating thoughtful planning.** The choice of spectrophotometry and growth metrics is appropriate to the RQ, and tables specify independent, dependent and controlled variables. 
â¢ However, some shortcomingsâthree-week duration may be too brief for full mycorrhizal colonisation, colonisation was not verified microscopically, and the stated 12 pots conflict with 15 replicates in the dataâshow **partial, not comprehensive, completeness**. 
â¢ Rationale for sample size and statistical power is implied but not explicitly discussed. These gaps place the work in the 3-4 band, at the upper end (4).

**The student draws conclusions aligned with data and identifies strengths, weaknesses and limitations, proposing sensible improvements** (longer duration, colonisation checks, soluble P source). 
â¢ Evaluation, however, remains somewhat **general and occasionally superficial**; for example, the impact of methodological flaws on specific results is noted but not quantified, and broader implications are asserted rather than critically weighed. 
â¢ Thus the discussion is adequate and often well-supported but lacks the sustained depth characteristic of the higher bands, fitting best at 4 within the 4-6 range.

**The research question is explicitly stated, focused and operational.** It identifies the independent variables (fungal inoculation and two phosphorus levels) and the measurable dependent variables (height, leaf count, P uptake) across a defined three-week period. 
â¢ The scope is realistic for an EE: a single plant species, two nutrient regimes and manageable time-frame; it addresses a genuine biological and agronomic issue. 
â¢ Some complexity (two P levels Ã inoculation) is handled, though the question might be refined further to emphasise the interaction effect; nonetheless, it meets the descriptors for the top band, meriting a score of 5 rather than 6.

**Discipline-specific terminology is used accurately and with consistency** (e.g., arbuscule, extramatrical hyphae, molybdenum blue method, Tukey HSD, relative growth rate). 
â¢ Concepts such as nutrient mobility, symbiosis, and ANOVA assumptions are applied correctly, showing secure understanding. Occasional grammatical slips do not impede clarity or accuracy. 
â¢ The sustained, correct use of biological and statistical vocabulary meets the 5-6 band; minor lapses suggest a score of 5.

annotations.comment: Step 6 in the procedure is incomplete (âtransfer the soil to a putâ), reducing clarity; instructions must be fully specified.
The standard deviation table on this page lacks contextâreaders cannot discern which dataset or variable each SD refers to.
The ANOVA discussion notes large betweenâgroup SS versus withinâgroup SS but does not quantify effect size (e.g., Î·Â²), which is essential for interpreting practical significance.
The Tukey HSD calculation displays â= 0. â¡â instead of the computed value, leaving a critical step incomplete.
The inclusion of a Word Count (3997) demonstrates compliance with IB formatting requirements, ensuring the student does not exceed the 4000-word limit.
No visible page number is provided in the footer, which fails to meet the requirement for numbered pages and hinders navigation.
Two redundant headings (âTable of Contentsâ and âContentsâ) create unnecessary duplication; consolidating these would improve clarity.
The introduction contains repetitive phrasing (e.g., âenhance our knowledgeâ) that could be tightened to communicate the topic more effectively.
The research question is clear but could be refined to emphasise the specific interaction effect between inoculation and phosphorus level.
Soil pH monitoring is described but no pH measurements are reported; without data confirming pH control, treatment consistency is uncertain.
Materials list 12 pots, yet 15 replicates are analysed in the data; this inconsistency should be resolved in the methodology description.
Indentation and alignment in the table of contents are inconsistent, making it harder to follow the hierarchy of sections.
Citation â(M, 2008)â lacks full author details, reducing precision in source attribution; a complete reference should be provided.
Figure 1 lacks a scale bar and clear in-figure labels to contextualise dimensions, limiting its usefulness for the reader.
The variables table lists factors but does not explain the rationale for selecting these specific independent and dependent variables.
The equation layout omits units for molar mass and uses inconsistent formatting; align and label units clearly for each calculation.
The calibration plot (Figure 2) does not report an RÂ² value, which is critical for assessing the linearity and reliability of the standard curve.
Risk assessment prescribes neutralization of waste but omits detailed disposal protocols for acids and peroxides, which is essential for safety compliance.
Tables 2â4 are presented without explicit references in the text; the narrative should guide the reader through the tables by number.
The Leaf Count table header leaves an empty column label, reducing readability; all columns should have meaningful headings.
Box plot summary (Table 12) introduces âlower boundâ and âupper boundâ without explaining how they were calculated (e.g., Qâ â 1.5Â·IQR).
Figure 3âs caption omits definition of the error bars; a caption should state whether they represent standard deviation, standard error, or confidence intervals.
The height growth rate formula is labelled âHGRâ but later referred to as âRGR,â causing inconsistency in terminology.
Concluding that small standard deviations imply no outliers misapplies the concept; outlier detection requires specific criteria beyond spread alone.
The qualitative section incorrectly refers to âFigure 2â for I+HP when it should cite the corresponding image number, causing reader confusion.
